title: narcissus × fosteri subsp. consolationis
intro: Narcissus × fosteri subsp. consolationis, also known as Foster's narcissus or wild daffodil, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, derived from the cross between Narcissus pseudonarcissus and Narcissus obesus. This plant contains alkaloids, including galantamine, which possess medicinal properties, particularly in treating Alzheimer's disease and other cognitive disorders. The health benefits of Narcissus × fosteri subsp. consolationis include its potential to improve memory, reduce anxiety and stress, and boost cognitive function. The herbal preparations of this plant, such as extracts and tinctures, can be used to create remedies, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal supplement.
